5ea77f5cf1fe.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
5ea77f5cf1fe.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
5ea77f5cf1fe.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

b93dadba9904.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
b93dadba9904.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

018f5f021316.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
018f5f021316.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
018f5f021316.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

2d733b2a7889.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
2d733b2a7889.description: Your 4.7/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P40.0). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
2d733b2a7889.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

44c55b7743fa.title: Ranked #4 of 6 locally — peers are ahead
44c55b7743fa.description: At percentile 40.0, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

217b635eca52.title: Protect the 4.7/5 rating — it took years to build
217b635eca52.description: With 1669 reviews at 4.7/5, your discovery advantage is substantial. Protect it by responding to every review within 48 hours and prompting satisfied tables for feedback.
